caveats: a: BLS occupation data cover 519200 and include libraries and archives, although current payroll data indicate most 519 employment is in 51929.
a: The industry is already software- and algorithm-intensive, so gross task exposure can overstate the share that remains not yet automated.
a: The frozen labor share uses ancestor 519 data with LOW quality and may be distorted by libraries, archives, very large search platforms, and a 2024-wage versus 2022-receipts vintage mismatch.
rho: No representative source measures AI labor realization in 519290.
rho: Large public portals have engineering resources, data, and scale that lower-middle-market firms may lack.
rho: AI investment can improve relevance, coverage, and product breadth without reducing labor.
e: The frozen n input is missing, so the number of lower-middle-market firms is not defensibly known.
e: NAICS 519290 combines search portals with clipping, stock-photo, title-search, and recorded-information services.
e: A recurring digital revenue stream may still be unsuitable if it depends on one traffic platform, thin content rights, heavy paid acquisition, or a founder's technical knowledge.
s5: Gallup covers all employer-business owners and measures intentions rather than completed 519290 control sales.
s5: Public-company acquisition activity demonstrates strategic interest but does not provide a representative denominator or completion probability.
s5: Digital assets, data sets, domains, or teams may be sold without a qualifying whole-company control transfer.
q: No representative source measures automation-benefit retention in 519290.
q: Fixed subscriptions, cancellable monthly packages, CPC advertising, licensing, API usage, and transaction fees have different pass-through mechanisms.
q: Compute, licensed data, content protection, and product reinvestment can absorb gross labor benefits.
d5: BLS projects employment for 519000, including libraries and archives, rather than constant-price 519290 service quantity.
d5: Public marketplace filings do not represent clipping, stock-photo, title-search, and recorded-information services.
d5: Growth in information consumption or API use can coexist with declines in portal visits, ad clicks, or operator revenue.
o: An AI answer may bypass the customer-facing portal while still depending on an upstream data or content operator.
o: Yelp reports that search-result placement affects traffic and that AI products have used platform content without permission, but one large local-search platform is not representative.
o: Operator need is likely higher for proprietary, regulated, time-sensitive, or rights-controlled data than for general web discovery and commoditized information.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Business customers, advertisers, data licensees, and users pay fixed or automatically renewing subscriptions, listing and marketplace packages, data or API licensing fees, usage charges, transaction or referral fees, and impression-, click-, or performance-based advertising; free user access often supplies audience, queries, reviews, or other data that supports monetization.
lens.excluded_activities: Libraries and archives; content publishers and gaming-site publishers; news syndicates; social networks and media streaming distributors; internet access, web hosting, data processing, and customer-content formatting; firms whose principal activity belongs in software publishing, advertising services, or another information code; shells, captive internal tools, non-control financing vehicles, and firms outside the target normalized-EBITDA band.
lens.included_activities: U.S. lower-middle-market firms in NAICS 519290 repeatedly operating niche or general web search portals or providing recurring and repeat information services, including searchable databases, news-clipping services, stock-photo agencies, title-search services other than real estate, and telephone-based recorded information services.

a.bridge: Source population/quantity: May 2023 OEWS for broad 519200 reported computer and mathematical occupations at 36.54% of employment, management 14.94%, business and financial operations 11.71%, sales 11.45%, office and administrative support 10.84%, and arts and media 2.49%; official and company sources describe searchable databases, advertising, subscriptions, data licensing, and APIs. Transformation: mapped substantial portions of coding, analysis, classification, campaign operations, sales support, content processing, customer support, and administration to exposed tasks while discounting for existing automation and retaining human shares for strategy, security, proprietary-data rights, relationships, and quality accountability. Error direction: inclusion of libraries may understate digital exposure, while the presence of highly automated search platforms and unmeasured exact-industry task mix may overstate remaining opportunity.
caveats: BLS occupation data cover 519200 and include libraries and archives, although current payroll data indicate most 519 employment is in 51929.
The industry is already software- and algorithm-intensive, so gross task exposure can overstate the share that remains not yet automated.
The frozen labor share uses ancestor 519 data with LOW quality and may be distorted by libraries, archives, very large search platforms, and a 2024-wage versus 2022-receipts vintage mismatch.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task and time study from exact 519290 firms, split among search marketplaces, data and API services, clipping or image services, and title search, and separating existing algorithmic automation from new generative-AI capability, would materially change the range.
a.rationale: Software development, data ingestion and classification, search-quality analysis, content tagging and moderation, advertising operations, sales research, campaign setup, customer support, documentation, reporting, and routine administration are exposed to AI substitution or avoidable hiring. Product strategy, proprietary-data acquisition, security, complex engineering, customer relationships, rights enforcement, and accountable quality decisions remain less exposed.

d5.bridge: Source population/quantity: BLS projects employment in broad 519000 from 183,800 in 2024 to 206,500 in 2034, a 12.3% increase; Census and company filings identify search, subscription, advertising, data, and API service baskets. Transformation: treated the broad employment path as a directional demand anchor, converted it to a five-year center, and widened the range for productivity, libraries, business-model mix, AI-assisted demand growth, direct-answer substitution, and traffic changes. Error direction: employment can understate demand when software productivity rises and inclusion of libraries can bias the exact-industry path either way, while traffic-based measures can overstate durable paid demand.
caveats: BLS projects employment for 519000, including libraries and archives, rather than constant-price 519290 service quantity.
Public marketplace filings do not represent clipping, stock-photo, title-search, and recorded-information services.
Growth in information consumption or API use can coexist with declines in portal visits, ad clicks, or operator revenue.
d5.change_evidence: A five-year constant-price series for exact 519290 covering paid searches, active subscribers, API calls, licensed records or images, advertiser demand, qualified referrals, and recurring customers, with AI-generated answer and traffic-acquisition effects separated, would materially change the estimate.
d5.rationale: Demand for searchable, current, specialized, and machine-readable information should grow as digital activity and AI applications expand, and BLS projects broad 519 employment growth. Current portal clicks and advertising inventory can nonetheless decline when search engines or AI assistants answer directly, consumer habits shift, or content is scraped and redistributed; clipping, image, and title-search niches may move differently.

caveats: The frozen n input is missing, so the number of lower-middle-market firms is not defensibly known.
NAICS 519290 combines search portals with clipping, stock-photo, title-search, and recorded-information services.
A recurring digital revenue stream may still be unsuitable if it depends on one traffic platform, thin content rights, heavy paid acquisition, or a founder's technical knowledge.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level 519290 dataset covering revenue by subscription, advertising, licensing, usage, and transactions; normalized EBITDA; customer and traffic concentration; proprietary data rights; headcount; ownership; and correct NAICS classification would materially change the estimate.
e.rationale: Searchable data, recurring audience demand, automatically renewing marketplace packages, data licensing, APIs, clipping, and repeat title or image search can fit the recurring external-service lens. Eligibility is reduced by the missing firm-count anchor, advertising-only or consumer-scale portals, founder or key-engineer dependence, traffic concentration, weak proprietary rights, unprofitable growth, platform dependence, cybersecurity exposure, customer concentration, and business models classified near software, publishing, advertising, or data processing.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 519 ($54,442,667,789) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 519 ($326,617,849,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: ancestor 519. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1728 (margins.json[51]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Information Services EBITDA/Sales 17.28% (conservative sector default)); revenue/employee $835,402 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 519); DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE
n.rationale: dataset n_band has no defensible value

caveats: An AI answer may bypass the customer-facing portal while still depending on an upstream data or content operator.
Yelp reports that search-result placement affects traffic and that AI products have used platform content without permission, but one large local-search platform is not representative.
Operator need is likely higher for proprietary, regulated, time-sensitive, or rights-controlled data than for general web discovery and commoditized information.
o.change_evidence: User and customer cohorts comparing standalone portals and information vendors with AI assistants, direct-answer search, data-owner APIs, and self-service tools on query completion, paid conversion, accuracy, provenance, latency, rights, and repeat use would materially change the estimate.
o.rationale: Proprietary databases, current local or specialized information, data licenses, content rights, advertiser and customer relationships, quality controls, security, and liability still require accountable operators. However, general queries, routine research, basic classification, and commoditized content can be answered or assembled by AI assistants, search-engine result pages, self-service APIs, direct data owners, or scraped content without a standalone portal relationship.

caveats: No representative source measures automation-benefit retention in 519290.
Fixed subscriptions, cancellable monthly packages, CPC advertising, licensing, API usage, and transaction fees have different pass-through mechanisms.
Compute, licensed data, content protection, and product reinvestment can absorb gross labor benefits.
q.change_evidence: Matched customer-contract, advertising-auction, API-usage, renewal, churn, traffic-acquisition, data-rights, cloud, model, labor, and contribution-margin data before and after AI deployment across business models would materially change the estimate.
q.rationale: Fixed-price subscriptions, data licenses, APIs, and marketplace packages can preserve implemented savings until renewal, while auction or performance advertising can convert better relevance and support efficiency into margin or volume. Retention is limited by month-to-month cancellation, auction pricing, competitive feature matching, customer ROI demands, falling traffic, revenue sharing, data and content royalties, cloud and model costs, and reinvestment required to keep search quality and security competitive.

caveats: No representative source measures AI labor realization in 519290.
Large public portals have engineering resources, data, and scale that lower-middle-market firms may lack.
AI investment can improve relevance, coverage, and product breadth without reducing labor.
rho.change_evidence: Pre/post deployment evidence from exact-industry firms covering engineering and support hours, release cadence, data-processing cost, search quality, conversion, moderation errors, rights claims, security incidents, customer churn, and model or cloud expense would materially change the estimate.
rho.rationale: Digitally native firms can integrate coding copilots, retrieval and classification models, automated campaign tools, customer-service agents, content processing, and analytic workflows within five years. Realization is constrained by search and recommendation quality, hallucination, data rights, privacy, security, model and cloud cost, latency, legacy architecture, advertiser trust, customer-specific integrations, and the need for human review of high-impact output.

s5.bridge: Source population/quantity: Gallup's fall-2024 survey reported that 22% of employer-business owners planned to sell, transfer, or take public within five years; Yelp discloses acquisition of complementary technology and businesses as part of its strategy. Transformation: used the broad 22% intention rate as an upper anchor, then discounted for noncompletion, founder or technical-person dependence, data-rights and cybersecurity diligence, traffic concentration, rapid product obsolescence, and non-control asset or team transactions. Error direction: broad intentions overstate completed qualifying transfers, while active strategic demand for proprietary data, audience, and technology may make the low bound conservative.
caveats: Gallup covers all employer-business owners and measures intentions rather than completed 519290 control sales.
Public-company acquisition activity demonstrates strategic interest but does not provide a representative denominator or completion probability.
Digital assets, data sets, domains, or teams may be sold without a qualifying whole-company control transfer.
s5.change_evidence: Closed-control transaction counts and eligible-firm denominators from digital-media and information-services brokers, PitchBook or Capital IQ, SEC and state merger filings, and marketplace or data-business cohorts over five years would materially change the estimate.
s5.rationale: Digital information businesses with subscriptions, advertiser relationships, data assets, APIs, or specialized audiences can transfer control, and complementary technology or data acquisitions are a plausible exit path. Completion is discounted for broad owner intentions, founder dependence, weak or disputed content rights, traffic and platform concentration, cybersecurity liabilities, fast technology change, and acquihires or asset purchases that do not transfer the whole firm.

principal_driver: The principal driver is a digitally native, high-wage workflow base in which AI can improve engineering, data processing, search, advertising, sales, and support at scale.
principal_weakness: The principal weakness is that AI assistants and direct-answer search can bypass the portal, reuse its information, and compress the value of traffic-dependent or commoditized services.
